## 3. Decisions (ADR Log)

<!-- Architecture Decision Records — log all significant decisions -->
<!-- Format: ADR-NNN: Title -->

| ID | Date | Decision | Status | Context |
|----|------|----------|--------|---------|
| ADR-001 | 2026-06-24 | Migrate via booting a real KK container per hop (Liquibase L1 + RealmMigration L2), not pure-SQL | Accepted | L2 RealmMigration runs only on server start; SQL export misses it |
| ADR-002 | 2026-06-24 | Hop path 16→24.0.5→26.6.3 (target 26) / 16→25.0.6 (target 25); forbid 26.6.0/26.6.1; PG≥14 for 26 | Accepted | Migrations cumulative (researched, 18 sources); fewer boots safer; avoid #48438/#47908 |
| ADR-003 | 2026-06-24 | KK images built FROM Astra/RedOS base; overridable image ref; offline tar load | Accepted | Sovereign OS requirement, air-gapped/banking delivery, branded images |
| ADR-004 | 2026-06-24 | CONTAINER_RUNTIME abstraction (podman/docker autodetect); single-host `run` topology added | Accepted | Astra/RedOS ship podman; tool was docker-hardcoded |
| ADR-005 | 2026-06-24 | Authoritative success gate = MIGRATION_MODEL advance (Layer 2), tightened wait + fail-closed | Accepted | "Container started" ≠ "migration applied"; old regex too loose |
| ADR-006 | 2026-06-26 | Air-gap bundle (extends ADR-003): build 8 images (16.1.1/24.0.5/25.0.6/26.6.3 × Astra SE/RED OS) FROM operator-supplied licensed bases → PRIVATE GHCR + PRIVATE Release tar; recipes/tooling PUBLIC, bases NEVER in repo; file-config (config/images.conf) for build/use/branded images | Accepted | Sovereign air-gap delivery + licence boundary; images can't live in git (size/licence) |
| ADR-007 | 2026-06-27 | Reduce manual setup: env-precedence for PROFILE_CONTAINER_IMAGE_REF/IMAGE_TAR/BASE_IMAGE in profile_load (use `<os>-<version>` tags directly, no re-tag); non-interactive `--yes`/`ASSUME_DEFAULTS` with a fail-closed main gate; one-shot `migrate_oneshot.sh`; config_wizard gains run+acquisition | Accepted | Real migration should be one command; the ':'-ref clobber forced a brittle re-tag; CI/automation need non-interactive runs without silently migrating prod |
| ADR-008 | 2026-07-14 | State is a FACT, not a journal: reconcile against reality (MIGRATION_MODEL version + DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK + live containers) BEFORE deciding hops; checkpoints and the profile's current_version are downgraded to claims/audit-trail | Accepted | Trusting the journal caused real damage on a live run: a stale checkpoint made the tool "skip the start" of a container that no longer existed and then wait out a 900s timeout; the profile's claimed current_version would also have re-run hops the DB had already passed |
| ADR-011 | 2026-07-15 | **One migration per database, isolated — not just detected.** A PostgreSQL SESSION advisory lock (`pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtext('kc-migrate:'||current_database()))`), held for the whole run by a persistent psql coproc, is the authority on "who is migrating this database": a second run (any work dir, any host) is refused immediately, and the lock auto-releases if the run crashes (connection drop). Transient container names become `kc-migrate-<version>-<db-token>` so runs against DIFFERENT databases proceed in parallel without colliding. `psql` absent → degrade to the file lock with a warning | Accepted | The per-workspace file lock only caught a re-run from the same work dir; two runs against one database from different work dirs corrupted its schema, and the container name `kc-migrate-<version>` was global to the daemon so different-DB runs fought over it. The database is the only correct arbiter of database ownership |
| ADR-010 | 2026-07-14 | **Layer 3: the data is a gate too.** A baseline of `COUNT(*)` on realm/user_entity/client/keycloak_role is taken ONCE before the first hop; every hop is then checked against it (realm+user unchanged, client+role may only grow). A violation fails the hop and offers the rollback. Backups may be PROVEN restorable (`PROFILE_VERIFY_BACKUP_RESTORE`) by restoring into a scratch DB and comparing counts. Post-migration acceptance is the `verify` subcommand: boots the target sovereign image against the migrated DB with health enabled, checks L2+L3+readiness+Admin API | Accepted | L1 and L2 prove the migration RAN; neither proves the data SURVIVED it. A hop that emptied `user_entity` passed every existing check and reported success. The policy already existed but guarded only the synthetic harness runs — the real migrations were unguarded. "Verifying" a backup meant parsing its table of contents, which is not the same as being able to restore it |
| ADR-009 | 2026-07-14 | **Health is DIAGNOSTIC; L2 is the gate.** A hop's success is decided by MIGRATION_MODEL (ADR-005) and by nothing else. A failed/unreachable health probe produces a warning and never triggers a rollback; the rollback offer moves to the L2 gate alone and defaults to NO. `health_check` returns a three-valued code (OK / NOT_SERVED / UNCONFIRMED) and probes the port the version actually serves (KC≥25 → :9000/health/ready, KC<25 → :8080/health). Real post-migration acceptance moves to the `verify` subcommand | Accepted | The probe hardcoded `:8080/health`, which KC 24+ serves only with `KC_HEALTH_ENABLED=true` and KC≥25 moved to port 9000 — so it 404'd on **every** supported hop. A 404 was read as "unhealthy", and an unhealthy check rolled the migration back — over a hop L2 had already CONFIRMED. `_confirm`'s "Y" default auto-answers YES in any non-TTY (CI, cron, pipe) and under `--yes`, which `migrate_oneshot` always passes: silent, unattended restore-from-backup of a **successful** migration. `run` mode had an early `return 0` papering over it; the other five deployment modes carried the live defect |
| ADR-012 | 2026-07-21 | **pg-client autonomy: host psql/pg_dump/pg_restore no longer required.** Every client call routes through `pg_client` (scripts/lib/container_runtime.sh): host binary if present (byte-identical fast path, keeps -Fd/-j), else the tool runs inside `$PROFILE_PG_CLIENT_IMAGE` via `cr run --rm -i --network=host` with PGPASSWORD forwarded and host files bind-mounted (`PG_CLIENT_MOUNT`, same path) so -Fd/-j survive; `--user` only on rootful docker. Dependency gates use `pg_client_available` (host binary OR `cr image inspect`). db_lock's long-lived advisory-lock coproc IS containerized when host psql is absent (v3.9.7 full parity — coproc over `run --rm -i`, released via `cr rm -f`), so there is NO degradation to the per-workspace file lock; crash-release works via stdin-EOF (live-validated on docker). Known limitation: a 2nd CONCURRENT container-lock acquire can stall then fail closed (still refuses) under `docker run -i` | Accepted | Sovereign/air-gap nodes may ship only a container engine; requiring host postgresql-client on the migration node contradicts the zero-host-mutation goal. Streaming was rejected for the file paths because -Fd/-j need a seekable file; a same-path bind-mount preserves both paths' behaviour |
| ADR-013 | 2026-07-21 | **pg-client image is sovereign by default (extends ADR-006).** `PROFILE_PG_CLIENT_IMAGE` defaults to a per-OS image built FROM the ALSE / RED OS base + `postgresql-client` of the SERVER major (not the Keycloak version), built via `containerfiles/Containerfile.pgclient` + `build_matrix.sh --pgclient` FROM the OS ubi base + `postgresql-client-<major>` from **PGDG** (the sovereign Astra 1.8.5 repo caps at client-15 — too old to `pg_dump` a PG16+ server; PGDG packages are pulled at BUILD time only, the image is self-contained and ships in the air-gap bundle). `migrate_oneshot` defaults `PROFILE_PG_CLIENT_IMAGE` to `<ns>:<os>-pgclient-<major>`; upstream `postgres:<major>` stays the override/fallback. Client major must be ≥ DB server major (pg_dump refuses a newer server). Implemented v3.9.7: `astra-pgclient-17` (168MB) built + live-validated (full autonomous 16→24→26 vs a PG16 server); RED OS variant authored (dnf/PGDG autodetect), build on a RED OS host/runner | Accepted | A Debian-based `postgres:16` default drags a non-sovereign ~450MB image into an ALSE/RED OS air-gap contour and would fail against a PG 17/18 server; the delivery's whole premise is sovereign bases |
| ADR-014 | 2026-07-22 | **Air-gap bundle carries the sovereign pg-client + per-OS private prereleases (extends ADR-006/ADR-013).** `build_bundle.sh` auto-includes `kc-<os>-pgclient-<major>.tar` (nullglob, OPTIONAL — absence is a warning, not a stop) beside the four KC hop tarballs; `migrate_oneshot.sh --source bundle` DISCOVERS it by glob (the bundle is the source of truth for which major shipped), loads it best-effort (a corrupt/absent tar degrades to host psql, never aborts), and — unless the caller pinned `PROFILE_PG_CLIENT_IMAGE` — repoints it at exactly the loaded image, so a fully air-gapped node with no host psql obtains the client from the bundle regardless of the built major. The `versions` workflow input is a single source of truth: the same set feeds `build_matrix` and `build_bundle` (via `--versions`), and per-OS prerelease tags carry `run_id-run_attempt` so a re-run does not collide. `build-images.yml` builds `--pgclient`, packs ONE bundle per OS, and publishes TWO private per-OS prereleases (astra→`alsebased`, redos→`redosbased`) instead of one combined Release; a bundle over GitHub's 2 GiB asset cap is uploaded as a CI artifact but NOT attached (out-of-band delivery from the runner's `dist/`) — never split, never fail | Accepted | v3.9.7 made pg-client autonomy the prod default, yet the offline bundle still shipped only KC images — a truly isolated node without host psql had no way to obtain the client. Per-OS prereleases keep each licensed-OS bundle separable and each under the asset cap independently (the RED OS bundle with pg-client can exceed 2 GiB) |
| ADR-015 | 2026-07-22 | **Rootless engine + non-root-everywhere + DB connection string/schema.** The pg-client image runs non-root (`USER 1000`) like the KC hops, so nothing runs as container-root. The tool auto-detects a rootless engine (`cr_is_rootless`) and adapts: rootless Podman + a bind mount gets `--userns=keep-id` (a non-root container writes caller-owned dumps), and a rootless-Docker + loopback DB host is rewritten to `host.docker.internal` via `host-gateway` (a rootless dockerd's `--network=host` is its own netns, not the machine's). `migrate_oneshot` gains `--db-url` (DSN; discrete flags override; a URL password warns) and `--db-schema` (`PGOPTIONS=-c search_path=<schema>,public` forwarded to every tool query + `KC_DB_SCHEMA` on the containers). The rootful path is byte-identical; Podman is the sovereign default and is rootless-native | Accepted | Sovereign air-gap security posture requires no root on the host and none inside containers; the target DB may sit on a non-default port or schema, or be supplied as a single connection string |
<!-- | ADR-001 | 2026-01-15 | Use PostgreSQL for persistence | Accepted | Need ACID, complex queries | -->

## 6. Change Log

<!-- Significant architectural changes (auto-updated by Claude Code) -->

| Date | Change | ADR | Author |
|------|--------|-----|--------|
| 2026-06-24 | v3.7 container-hop migration: runtime abstraction, image acquisition (pull/load/preloaded/build), build helper, run topology, Layer-2 verification | ADR-001..005 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-06-26 | Air-gap packaging Phase A: Containerfile.kc16 (WildFly), build_matrix.sh (dry-run/build/publish + config/images.conf + USE-branded), build-images.yml (dispatch/self-hosted GHCR+Release), AIRGAP.md | ADR-006 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-06-27 | v3.9 automation: IMAGE_REF env-precedence (no re-tag), non-interactive `--yes`/`ASSUME_DEFAULTS` (fail-closed gate), `migrate_oneshot.sh`, config_wizard run+acquisition, profile_save acquisition/runtime, 5 new test suites | ADR-007 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-14 | v3.9.1 state reconciliation: `kc_reconcile_state()` reads MIGRATION_MODEL / DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK / live containers before planning hops; already-applied hops are skipped; stale Liquibase lock detected (`--force-unlock`); checkpoints verified against reality (`--no-resume`) | ADR-008 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-14 | v3.9.2 the five deployment modes other than `run` never received the v3.9.1 fixes and carried data-loss defects: a 404 health probe rolled back an L2-confirmed migration (unattended, via `_confirm`'s "Y" default); checkpoints outlived the rollback that undid them; the docker/podman image update destroyed the user's container before discovering it could not rebuild it; hop backups were written to a directory rotation never swept; Ctrl-C left production stopped (`--replicas=0`) with nobody restoring it | ADR-009 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-15 | Concurrency isolation: competing-process detection made precise (argv-element match, exclude `$$`+`$BASHPID`, no PPID walk — it had flagged its own subshell/launcher and aborted every run); per-database advisory lock (one migration per DB, cross-host, auto-releasing); per-database transient container names (parallel-safe for different DBs) | ADR-011 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-21 | v3.9.7 pg-client autonomy: host psql/pg_dump/pg_restore optional, routed via `pg_client`/`pg_client_available` (container fallback, `PG_CLIENT_MOUNT` bind-mount, docker-only `--user`); 6 libs self-source container_runtime.sh; new test_pg_client suite (31/31 green); sovereign pg-client image default decided, build pending on sovereign runner | ADR-012, ADR-013 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-22 | Air-gap delivery: sovereign pg-client rides IN the per-OS bundle (`build_bundle.sh` auto-include + `migrate_oneshot.sh --source bundle` best-effort load) + `build-images.yml` per-OS private prereleases (`alsebased`/`redosbased`) + 2 GiB size-guard (out-of-band over cap, never split/fail); new inputs `pg_client_major`/`build_pgclient`; AIRGAP.md §5 rewritten; tests extended (build_bundle, oneshot); VERSION unchanged (CI/delivery only) | ADR-014 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-22 | Fix: containerized advisory-lock release hang (`db_lock.sh` `_kc_db_lock_release`) — `cr rm -f` the lock container BEFORE reaping the `docker run -i` coproc (was after a blocking `wait`), + bounded SIGTERM→SIGKILL→wait. Autonomous `16→24→26` went 900s-hang(rc=124) → clean rc=0/~108s. Regression guards in test_db_lock.sh | ADR-011/012 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-22 | Rootless/non-root: pg-client image `USER 1000` (astra+redos); `cr_is_rootless` detect + rootless-podman `--userns=keep-id` + rootless-docker host-loopback rewrite (`host.docker.internal`); `migrate_oneshot --db-url` (DSN) + `--db-schema` (PGOPTIONS search_path + KC_DB_SCHEMA); docs/ROOTLESS.md; hermetic tests. Live-validated on rootless Podman (dump/restore, caller-owned); rootless-Docker hermetically tested (sudo-gated live) | ADR-015 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-22 | **Release v3.10.0** — `VERSION` 3.9.7 → 3.10.0, changelog cut for PR #18-24 (sovereign pg-client image ADR-013, air-gap bundle ADR-014, non-root pg-client + `--db-url`/`--db-schema`, rootless engine mode + streaming ADR-015, preflight net-defer + db_lock release fixes). All non-root images (astra+redos hops 16/24/25/26 + both pg-clients, `USER 1000`) pushed to ghcr; per-OS air-gap bundles rebuilt WITH pg-client and autonomously validated (16→24→26, `rc=0`, non-root pg-client loaded from bundle) | ADR-014, ADR-015 | @AlexGromer |
